title: carduncellus pinnatus subsp. matritensis
intro: Carduncellus pinnatus subsp. matritensis, also known as Cardoon, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and expectorant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Cardoon can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and it has also been used in traditional medicine as a tincture or in combination with other herbs for its medicinal properties.
